title: शिव आरती — ॐ जय शिव ओंकारा
titleEn: Shiv Aarti - Om Jai Shiv Omkara
devanagariText:
ॐ जय शिव ओंकारा, स्वामी जय शिव ओंकारा।
ब्रह्मा विष्णु सदाशिव, अर्द्धांगी धारा॥
ॐ जय शिव ओंकारा॥

एकानन चतुरानन पञ्चानन राजे।
हंसासन गरुड़ासन वृषवाहन साजे॥
ॐ जय शिव ओंकारा॥

दो भुज चार चतुर्भुज दसभुज अति सोहे।
तीनों रूप निरखता त्रिभुवन जन मोहे॥
ॐ जय शिव ओंकारा॥

अक्षमाला वनमाला मुण्डमाला धारी।
चन्दन मृगमद शोभित भोले शुभकारी॥
ॐ जय शिव ओंकारा॥

श्वेताम्बर पीताम्बर बाघम्बर अंगे।
सनकादिक ब्रह्मादिक भूतादिक संगे॥
ॐ जय शिव ओंकारा॥

कर मध्ये कमण्डलु चक्र त्रिशूलधारी।
सुखकारी दुखहारी जग पालनकारी॥
ॐ जय शिव ओंकारा॥

ब्रह्मा विष्णु सदाशिव जानत अविवेका।
प्रणवाक्षर में शोभित ये तीनों एका॥
ॐ जय शिव ओंकारा॥

त्रिगुण स्वामी जी की आरती जो कोई नर गावे।
कहत शिवानन्द स्वामी मनवांछित फल पावे॥
ॐ जय शिव ओंकारा॥
transliteration:
Om Jai Shiv Omkara, Swami Jai Shiv Omkara.
Brahma Vishnu Sadashiv, Ardhangi Dhara.
Om Jai Shiv Omkara.

Ekanan Chaturanan Panchanan Raje.
Hansasan Garudasan Vrishavahan Saje.
Om Jai Shiv Omkara.

Do Bhuj Char Chaturbhuj Dasbhuj Ati Sohe.
Tinon Roop Nirakhata Tribhuvan Jan Mohe.
Om Jai Shiv Omkara.

Akshmala Vanmala Mundmala Dhari.
Chandan Mrigmad Shobhit Bhole Shubhkari.
Om Jai Shiv Omkara.

Shvetambar Pitambar Baghambhar Ange.
Sankadik Brahmadik Bhootadik Sange.
Om Jai Shiv Omkara.

Kar Madhye Kamandalu Chakra Trishuldhari.
Sukhkari Dukh-hari Jag Palankari.
Om Jai Shiv Omkara.

Brahma Vishnu Sadashiv Janat Aviveka.
Pranavakshar Mein Shobhit Ye Tinon Eka.
Om Jai Shiv Omkara.

Trigun Swami Ji Ki Aarti Jo Koi Nar Gave.
Kahat Shivanand Swami Manvanchhit Phal Pave.
Om Jai Shiv Omkara.
hindiMeaning:
ॐकार स्वरूप शिव की जय हो। ब्रह्मा, विष्णु और सदाशिव — तीनों अपनी अर्धांगिनियों सहित विराजमान हैं।

एक मुख (ब्रह्मा — चार मुख — शिव — पाँच मुख) — तीनों विराजमान हैं। हंस, गरुड़ और वृषभ — तीनों के वाहन सज रहे हैं।

दो भुजा (ब्रह्मा), चार भुजा (विष्णु), दस भुजा (शिव) — तीनों रूप अत्यन्त शोभायमान हैं। इन तीनों रूपों को देखकर तीनों लोकों के जन मोहित हो जाते हैं।

रुद्राक्ष की माला, वनमाला और मुण्डमाला धारण करने वाले, चन्दन और कस्तूरी से शोभित, भोले शंकर शुभकारी हैं।

श्वेत वस्त्र, पीले वस्त्र और बाघम्बर धारण करते हैं। सनकादिक, ब्रह्मादिक और भूतगण — सभी उनके साथ हैं।

हाथ में कमण्डलु, चक्र और त्रिशूल धारण करते हैं। सुखकारी, दुःखहारी, जगत के पालनकर्ता हैं।

ब्रह्मा, विष्णु और सदाशिव — अविवेकी जन इन्हें अलग-अलग जानते हैं, किन्तु प्रणव (ॐ) में ये तीनों एक ही हैं।

त्रिगुण स्वामी की यह आरती जो भी गाता है, शिवानन्द स्वामी कहते हैं, वह मनवांछित फल प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires of the devotee who sings it with devotion
Most popular and widely sung aarti of Lord Shiva
Invokes the blessings of the Hindu Trinity — Brahma, Vishnu, and Mahesh
Ideal for evening Shiva puja and temple worship
Purifies the home environment and removes negativity
Traditionally associated with family harmony, peace, and spiritual upliftment
description: Dedicated to Lord Shiva, the Shiv Aarti - Om Jai Shiv Omkara (शिव आरती — ॐ जय शिव ओंकारा) is a traditional worship song (aarti) revered in Hindu tradition. This revered text originates from Traditional Hindu Aarti (attributed to Shivanand Swami). Devotees recite this prayer to bring family harmony, peace, and spiritual upliftment and purify the home environment and remove negativity. It is also said to invoke the blessings of the Hindu Trinity — Brahma, Vishnu, and Mahesh. This prayer is traditionally recited on Monday (Somvar) and Daily and during Maha Shivaratri and Shravan month to invoke divine grace. At 8 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 5 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Shiv Aarti - Om Jai Shiv Omkara in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Shiv Aarti Om Jai Shiv Omkara in Hindi with meaning and transliteration. The most popular Shiva aarti sung during evening puja.
source: Traditional Hindu Aarti (attributed to Shivanand Swami)

seoFaqs.question: Who wrote the Shiv Aarti 'Om Jai Shiv Omkara'?
seoFaqs.answer: The aarti was composed by Swami Shivananda of Varanasi in the late 19th century. Swami Vivekananda heard it being sung in Kashi and brought the hymn back to the Belur Math, from where it spread across India. The signature verse 'Kahat Shivanand Swami' at the close explicitly names the composer — a traditional Hindi convention called bhanita or chhap.

seoFaqs.question: What does 'Om Jai Shiv Omkara' mean?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening line means 'Glory to you, O Shiva, who are the very embodiment of Om (Omkara)'. The aarti identifies Shiva as the primordial sound, the threefold deity (Brahma-Vishnu-Mahesh in one form), and the trigunatmaka — the lord of the three gunas of sattva, rajas and tamas. Each verse maps a cosmic principle onto Shiva's iconography.

seoFaqs.question: When should Shiv Aarti be performed?
seoFaqs.answer: Shiv Aarti is sung at the close of every Shiva puja and Rudrabhishek. Traditional best times include Mondays (Somvar), Pradosh Kaal (twilight on the 13th lunar day), the entire month of Shravan, Mahashivratri (especially during all four prahars of the night), and during Kartik Purnima. Daily morning and Sandhya aarti are standard at every Shiva temple.

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between this aarti and the Ramakrishna Mission?
seoFaqs.answer: Although composed by Swami Shivananda of Varanasi (a separate figure from Swami Sivananda of Rishikesh), the aarti was popularised by Swami Vivekananda after he heard it sung on the ghats of Kashi. He instituted its daily singing at Belur Math, and it remains part of the official Ramakrishna Math sandhya aarti sequence — which is why it is one of the most widely-known Shiva hymns today.

seoFaqs.question: How does Om Jai Shiv Omkara differ from Shiv Tandav Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Om Jai Shiv Omkara is a short 5-verse aarti in simple Hindi, sung with a lit lamp at the close of puja — accessible to all ages. The Shiv Tandav Stotram, attributed to Ravana, is a 17-verse Sanskrit composition in the rapid Panchachamara metre, recited as a powerful stotra during meditation or Rudrabhishek rather than as a lamp-offering aarti.
